name: Railway Express Agency, Inc. v. New York
question: Did a New York City traffic ordinance prohibiting vehicles from displaying advertisements unrelated to the owner's business interests violate the Fourteenth Amendment Equal Protection Clause?
description: A case in which the Court decided that a city ordinance prohibiting commercial advertising on vehicles except in cases where those advertisements were related to the business interests of the owners was valid and did not violate the Equal Protection Clause of the Constitution.
